method_atom: Analysis of the analytic structure of a system's two-point response/correlation function in the complex plane of the conjugate variable (frequency/momentum), extracting a general principle that fixes where the function's singularities may lie. The method then tracks how the qualitative character of those singularities changes as a control (coupling) parameter is varied between two limiting regimes, in particular a change between branch points (cuts) and isolated simple poles. A strong/weak duality is used to map the otherwise intractable strong-coupling limit onto a tractable dual description.
object_atom: The two-point correlator of a relativistic finite-temperature quantum field theory at zero chemical potential, concretely N=4 supersymmetric Yang-Mills theory at large N_c, with the strong-coupling limit accessed via the AdS/CFT (gauge/gravity) correspondence.
brief_reason: What carries across is the complex-plane analyticity/singularity-structure analysis of a linear-response function and its weak-vs-strong-coupling comparison via a strong/weak duality; what is domain-specific is the thermal gauge theory (N=4 SYM) and its holographic AdS/CFT dual.
